_comment: DOME Registry annotated guide — v2.0.0. This file documents what each field should contain. In v2.0.0, all DOME content fields (D1–D4, O1–O8, M1–M4, E1–E5) are structured objects with typed sub-fields; sub-field values here are descriptive prompts, NOT example data. Use dome-registry-entry-template.json to create a blank submission, and dome-registry-entry-example.json to see a filled example. Fields marked [SYSTEM] are assigned server-side and should be omitted from new submissions.

publication.title: [Required] Full title of the publication exactly as it appears in the journal or preprint.
publication.authors: [Required] Author list as a single string, e.g. 'Smith A, Jones B, Müller C.'
publication.journal: [Required] Name of the journal, conference, or preprint server.
publication.pmcid: [Optional] PubMed Central identifier, if available, e.g. 'PMC8513558'.
publication.tags: [Optional] Array of free-text tags for the publication, e.g. ['protein', 'cancer', 'NLP'].

public: [Required] Boolean. Set to true to make this entry visible in the public DOME Registry.
isAiGenerated: [Required] Boolean. Set to true if this entry was generated automatically (e.g. by DOME Copilot); false if completed by a human annotator.
tags: [Optional] Array of free-text tags for the entry as a whole.

data._section: D — Data (4 fields, D1–D4). Describe the datasets used to train and test the method. Each field is a structured object with typed sub-fields.

availability.isPub: [D1.isPub] Boolean. Set to true if the dataset is publicly available, false if restricted or proprietary.
availability.licence: [D1.licence] The licence under which the dataset is distributed (e.g. 'CC BY 4.0', 'Proprietary', 'Not stated'). Provide 'Not stated' if no licence is declared.
availability.enfMeth: [D1.enfMeth] The URL, repository, or portal through which the dataset can be accessed (e.g. 'https://github.com/example-org/dataset'). State 'Not available' if access is restricted.

provenance._field: [D2] Origin and composition of the dataset.
provenance.source: [D2.source] Source database(s), study, or repository from which the dataset was derived. State the version or release date if known (e.g. 'BioLiP v2023-01', 'UniProt release 2023_04').
provenance.numDP: [D2.numDP] Total number of data points (samples/instances) in the full dataset prior to any splitting.
provenance.prevUse: [D2.prevUse] Boolean. Set to true if the dataset or a substantively similar benchmark has been used in prior published work. Cite relevant prior publications in the paper.

redundancy.splitMeth: [D3.splitMeth] Name of the tool or algorithm used to detect redundancy (e.g. 'cd-hit', 'BLAST', 'MMseqs2'). State 'Not reported' if redundancy was not assessed.
redundancy.isIndep: [D3.isIndep] Boolean. Set to true if training and test sets are guaranteed to contain no redundant or near-identical instances.
redundancy.enfMeth: [D3.enfMeth] The threshold or criterion used to define independence (e.g. '30% pairwise sequence identity', 'E-value < 1e-3'). State 'Not applied' if no threshold was enforced.
redundancy.distCmp: [D3.distCmp] The distance or similarity metric used to compare instances when assessing redundancy (e.g. 'pairwise sequence identity', 'structural RMSD', 'Tanimoto coefficient').

splits.numSplits: [D4.numSplits] Integer. The number of distinct splits defined (e.g. 5 for 5-fold CV, 3 for train/validation/test, 1 for a single train/test partition).
splits.dpPerSplit: [D4.dpPerSplit] Array of integers giving the number of data points in each split, in split order (e.g. [2690, 384, 768]).
splits.otherSplits: [D4.otherSplits] Description of any non-standard, additional, or nested splits applied (e.g. inner CV loop for hyperparameter selection within outer CV folds). State 'None' if not applicable.
splits.splitDist: [D4.splitDist] Strategy used to assign data points to splits (e.g. 'stratified random sampling maintaining class ratio', 'chronological split', 'cluster-based split to avoid homology leakage').

optimization._section: O — Optimisation (8 fields, O1–O8). Describe the training procedure, features, and hyperparameter tuning.

algorithm.algName: [O1.algName] Name and type of the ML algorithm(s) used (e.g. 'Random Forest classifier', 'LSTM neural network', 'Support Vector Machine with RBF kernel'). Provide a brief characterisation if the method is novel.
algorithm.isNewAlg: [O1.isNewAlg] Boolean. Set to true if the algorithm is novel and has not previously been published as a standalone method.
algorithm.unpubRsn: [O1.unpubRsn] If the algorithm is novel and unpublished, state the reason it is not yet independently published, or describe its relationship to prior work. Leave empty if isNewAlg is false.

config.cfgAvail: [O4.cfgAvail] Boolean. Set to true if the final hyperparameter settings (values used in the reported model) are stated in the paper or supplementary materials.
config.lic: [O4.lic] Licence under which the configuration file or associated code is released (e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache 2.0', 'Not stated'). State 'Not applicable' if no code is provided.

encoding.encMeth: [O3.encMeth] Method used to represent raw input data as numerical vectors for model input (e.g. 'one-hot encoding of amino acid sequence', 'PSSM from PSI-BLAST', 'pre-trained protein language model embeddings', 'graph Laplacian').

features._field: [O2] Input features used by the model.
features.numF: [O2.numF] Total number of input features (dimensionality of the feature vector presented to the model).
features.featSel: [O2.featSel] Boolean. Set to true if an explicit feature selection or dimensionality reduction step was applied.
features.trnOnlySel: [O2.trnOnlySel] Boolean. Set to true if feature selection was performed using only training-set data (i.e., no test-set information influenced feature selection, avoiding data leakage).

fit.pGtN: [O6.pGtN] Boolean. Set to true if the number of model parameters exceeds the number of training data points, indicating a potential overfitting risk.
fit.noOverFit: [O6.noOverFit] Brief statement of evidence that overfitting was not problematic (e.g. 'Training AUROC 0.97 vs test AUROC 0.83; gap consistent with RF behaviour on imbalanced data'). State 'Not assessed' if not addressed.
fit.noUnderFit: [O6.noUnderFit] Brief statement of evidence that underfitting was not problematic (e.g. 'Training AUROC 0.97 rules out underfitting'). State 'Not assessed' if not addressed.

meta.isMeta: [O8.isMeta] Boolean. Set to true if a meta-learning, ensemble, or model-combination strategy was used.
meta.metaMeths: [O8.metaMeths] Array of strings naming the ensemble or meta-learning methods used (e.g. ['bagging', 'stacking', 'boosting']). Leave as empty array if isMeta is false.
meta.metaIndep: [O8.metaIndep] Boolean. Set to true if the component models are trained independently (e.g. in bagging). Set to false if component models share information (e.g. in boosting). Leave null if isMeta is false.

parameters.numP: [O5.numP] Total number of free or learnable parameters in the final trained model. State null if not reported.
parameters.pSelMeth: [O5.pSelMeth] Method used to select or optimise parameters (e.g. 'grid search with 5-fold CV', 'Bayesian optimisation', 'manual tuning', 'default settings'). State 'Not reported' if not described.

regularisation.hasReg: [O7.hasReg] Boolean. Set to true if any regularisation technique was applied during training.
regularisation.regTechs: [O7.regTechs] Array of regularisation technique names applied (e.g. ['L2 weight decay', 'dropout', 'early stopping', 'data augmentation']). Leave as empty array if hasReg is false.

model._section: M — Model (4 fields, M1–M4). Describe the model and its availability.

availability.srcAvail: [M1.srcAvail] Boolean. Set to true if the model source code is publicly accessible (e.g. on GitHub, Zenodo, or a journal supplement).
availability.exeAvail: [M1.exeAvail] Boolean. Set to true if a trained model (weights, pickled model, Docker image, or web service) is publicly accessible.
availability.lic: [M1.lic] Licence under which the model or code is released (e.g. 'MIT', 'GPL-3.0', 'Apache 2.0', 'Proprietary'). State 'Not stated' if no licence is declared.

duration.runTime: [M4.runTime] Reported training duration and hardware context (e.g. '12 min on Intel Xeon E5-2680 CPU', '2 h on NVIDIA A100 GPU, 40 GB RAM'). State 'Not reported' if not given in the paper.

interpretability.interpType: [M2.interpType] Interpretability classification: 'transparent' (e.g. linear model, decision tree with reported coefficients), 'black box' (e.g. deep neural network without explanation), or 'post-hoc explainable' (explanation method applied after training).
interpretability.transpEx: [M2.transpEx] Description of the transparency or post-hoc explanation approach used (e.g. 'Gini feature importances reported for all features', 'SHAP values computed for test set', 'attention weights visualised'). State 'None' if the model is a black box with no explanation.

output.outType: [M3.outType] Precise description of the model output (e.g. 'binary classification per residue: binding (1) / non-binding (0) with probability in [0,1]', 'regression: predicted binding affinity in kcal/mol', 'multi-class probability vector over 20 amino acid classes').

evaluation._section: E — Evaluation (5 fields, E1–E5). Describe how model performance was assessed.

method.evalMeth: [E3.evalMeth] Evaluation protocol used to assess model performance (e.g. '5-fold stratified cross-validation on training set; final performance on independent held-out test set', 'leave-one-protein-out cross-validation', 'single train/test split').

availability.rawAvail: [E1.rawAvail] Boolean. Set to true if raw prediction scores, evaluation scripts, or result files are openly available (e.g. GitHub repository, Zenodo archive).
availability.lic: [E1.lic] Licence under which the evaluation data or code is released. State 'Not stated' if not declared.

comparison._field: [E4] Comparison to baselines or prior methods.
comparison.cmpPub: [E4.cmpPub] Boolean. Set to true if the method is benchmarked against at least one previously published state-of-the-art method on the same test set.
comparison.cmpBase: [E4.cmpBase] Boolean. Set to true if the method is compared to at least one naive or majority-class baseline.

confidance.hasCI: [E5.hasCI] Boolean. Set to true if confidence intervals, standard deviations across folds, or uncertainty estimates for performance metrics are reported.
confidance.isStatSig: [E5.isStatSig] Boolean. Set to true if formal statistical significance testing (e.g. paired t-test, Wilcoxon signed-rank test) was performed and results are reported.

measure.metrics: [E2.metrics] Array of performance metric names reported in the paper (e.g. ['AUROC', 'MCC', 'F1', 'precision', 'recall', 'accuracy']). Include all metrics mentioned.
measure.isRep: [E2.isRep] Boolean. Set to true if the evaluation is fully reproducible: all code, data, and instructions required to regenerate the reported results are publicly available.
